description: Iconic D&D monsters — the quintessential dungeon encounters and legendary threats.
creatures: Gnoll
A cackling, hyena-headed raider that fights in a frothing frenzy driven by endless hunger.
Ogre
A dim-witted, lumbering giant that smashes first and never bothers to think at all.
Troll
A lanky, rubber-skinned brute that regrows severed limbs and fears only acid and fire.
Giant
A towering humanoid that shakes the earth with each stride and hurls boulders like pebbles.
Beholder
A floating sphere of paranoid malice, covered in eyes that each fire a different deadly ray.
Mind Flayer
A tentacle-faced horror that cracks skulls open and devours brains while its victim still twitches.
Gelatinous Cube
A transparent, corridor-filling ooze that dissolves flesh on contact and displays its victims' bones within.
Owlbear
A feathered, bear-bodied brute with an owl's swiveling head and a blood-curdling screech.
Displacer Beast
A six-legged panther with barbed tentacles that projects illusions of itself a few feet from where it truly stands.
Rust Monster
A skittering, antenna-waving insect that corrodes metal on contact — a knight's worst nightmare.
Bulette
A massive, armored 'land shark' that burrows underground and explodes upward beneath its prey.
Tarrasque
A mountain-sized engine of destruction that levels cities and shrugs off the mightiest magic.
Mimic
A shapeshifting ambusher disguised as a treasure chest that sprouts teeth and a sticky tongue when touched.
Stirge
A blood-sucking flying pest with a needle-like proboscis that latches on and won't let go.
